key_insight: The paper extends compatible-strain mixed finite elements from simplicial meshes to general quadrilateral meshes for 2D nonlinear elasticity, preserving the differential-complex structure while supporting both compressible and incompressible formulations.
engineering_relevance: It enables more flexible and efficient quadrilateral discretizations for nonlinear solid mechanics, potentially reducing degrees of freedom without sacrificing compatibility.
method: Compute shape functions directly in physical space via numerical integration instead of relying on Piola transforms.
Use first-kind Nédélec elements to interpolate the displacement gradient.
Introduce compatible stress shape functions and an incompressible formulation with element-level pressure condensation.
evidence_signal: Numerical examples in the abstract indicate the quadrilateral elements can match problems requiring second-order simplicial CSMFEs while using fewer degrees of freedom.
watch_item: The approach depends on direct physical-space shape-function construction, so implementation may be more complex than standard mapped-element formulations, especially on general quadrilaterals.
paper_title: First-Order Compatible-Strain Mixed Quadrilateral Finite Elements for 2D Nonlinear Elasticity
